# Profiling Traces

This directory contains two Nsight Systems (`.nsys-rep`) traces.
**They were generated by two different scripts with different model architectures
and input sizes — they are not a controlled before/after experiment.**
See the table below for an exact accounting of what each trace represents.

---

## Hardware

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Node | g213 |
| GPUs | Dual NVIDIA H200 NVL |
| Interconnect | NVLink |
| OS | Rocky Linux 9.3 |
| Driver | 580.82.07 |

---

## Trace Inventory

| Trace | Script | Model | Input shape | Input elements | FSDP wrap | LoRA | Warmup | Steps profiled | NVTX range |
|-------|--------|-------|-------------|----------------|-----------|------|--------|----------------|------------|
| [`fsdp_baseline.nsys-rep`](fsdp_baseline.nsys-rep) | [`train/fsdp_profile.py`](../train/fsdp_profile.py) | 10× `nn.Linear(8192,8192)` | `[32, 8192]` | 262,144 | Default (whole model) | ❌ | ❌ | 1 | `FSDP_Step` |
| [`ifsdp_h200_nvlink_trace.nsys-rep`](ifsdp_h200_nvlink_trace.nsys-rep) | [`train/train_fsdp_lora.py`](../train/train_fsdp_lora.py) | 4× `LlamaStyleDecoderLayer` + LoRA | `[8, 2048, 8192]` | 134,217,728 | `ModuleWrapPolicy` per layer | ✅ rank=16 | ✅ 2 iters | ~12 | `End_to_End_FSDP_Optimization_Step` |

> **Note on input size:** the full run uses a 512× larger input
> (134M vs 262K elements), which directly explains the ~325× larger
> GEMM kernels (~92 ms vs ~283 µs) visible when comparing the two traces
> in Nsight. The NCCL timing difference is driven by input size and model
> architecture — not purely by the overlap mechanism.

---

## NCCL Communication — Full Run
(`ifsdp_h200_nvlink_trace.nsys-rep` only — the honest single-trace story)

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| AllGather avg per layer | 22.0 ms |
| ReduceScatter avg per layer | 22.9 ms |
| NCCL % of wall time | 3.2% |
| Typical GEMM kernel duration | 45–162 ms |
| Compute-to-communication ratio | ~30:1 |

**What this shows:**
With `ModuleWrapPolicy({LlamaStyleDecoderLayer})` each decoder layer is sharded
independently, so PyTorch FSDP can overlap the AllGather for layer N+1 with the
backward compute of layer N.
The result is that NCCL collectives (AllGather + ReduceScatter combined ~45 ms)
account for only **3.2% of wall time** against 45–162 ms GEMM kernels —
a compute-to-communication ratio of ~30:1 consistent with effective
layer-boundary overlap on dual H200 NVLink.

---

## Baseline Trace — Scope and Limitations
(`fsdp_baseline.nsys-rep`)

This trace was generated by [`train/fsdp_profile.py`](../train/fsdp_profile.py),
a minimal throwaway script used to verify Nsight tracing was working.
It uses **default FSDP wrapping** (whole model as one shard) which does **not**
enable layer-boundary compute-communication overlap — NCCL and compute are
serialised.
For that reason this trace should not be read as a baseline for the full run:
the FSDP parallelism strategy, model architecture, and input scale are all different.

---

## How to Open

```bash
# CLI stats — no GUI required
nsys stats profiles/ifsdp_h200_nvlink_trace.nsys-rep
nsys stats profiles/fsdp_baseline.nsys-rep

# Interactive GUI
nsys-ui profiles/ifsdp_h200_nvlink_trace.nsys-rep
```

NVTX ranges annotated in the traces:
- `End_to_End_FSDP_Optimization_Step`
- `Forward_Pass_Compute`
- `Backward_Pass_Compute_and_Comm_Overlap`
- `Optimizer_Parameter_Updates`
